description: Use when deciding whether to paste a screenshot vs text, sharing a UI bug or design reference with Claude, writing ASCII or Mermaid diagrams in project docs, sketching a UI mockup before a build session, or handling mobile / cross-platform visual comparisons. Routes to chapter 10 (visuals) of claude-spine.

results.query: I'm trying to describe a UI bug — the dropdown is overlapping the modal but only on iPhone SE in dark mode. Should I paste a screenshot or just describe it in text?
the architecture doc needs a diagram of how auth flows from client → middleware → Supabase. Mermaid or ASCII?
let me share this Figma design as a screenshot — Claude, build the navbar to match it pixel-by-pixel
I need to compare iOS vs Android rendering of this transaction list — both look 'right' but feel wrong on Android. How do I share that with you usefully?
the Mermaid library is throwing a parse error in our docs site — fix it
implement a screenshot tool for the support widget — capture the current page DOM
design a visual identity for the new SaaS — color palette and typography
before we build the dashboard, I want to sketch out the layout — give me an ASCII mockup of the three-column variant vs the sidebar-and-canvas variant
the visual regression tests are failing — pixel diff is 4% off on the pricing page
what's the difference between SVG and Canvas for rendering charts at scale
